name: Castillo de Luna – circular from Alburquerque
name_original: Castillo de Luna – circular desde Alburquerque
region_intro: Hiking around Alburquerque offers routes through a landscape characterized by historic castles, extensive cork oak forests, and designated conservation areas. The region provides a mix of terrain, from gentle paths suitable for leisurely walks to more challenging trails. This area, located near the Spanish-Portuguese border, features a blend of cultural landmarks and natural environments. Best hiking trails around Alburquerque The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Luna – circular from Alburquerque, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the historic surroundings of Alburquerque. Another top favourite among local hikers is Beautiful Cork Oak Forest loop from Esperança, a moderate 9.3 miles (15.1 km) path. This trail winds through serene cork oak woodlands, offering a tranquil natural experience. Local hikers also love the Caia Conservation Area loop from Campo Maior, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through a conservation area, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Alburquerque is defined by historic castles, cork oak forests, and conservation areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Alburquerque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Alburquerque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alburquerque?
A: Alburquerque offers a selection of 10 hiking trails, catering to various preferences. These include 4 easy routes, 5 moderate paths, and 1 more challenging trail, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Alburquerque?
A: Yes, Alburquerque has 4 easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed stroll. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991593688/castillo-de-luna-circular-from-alburquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Luna – circular from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.7 miles (5.9 km) long and takes about 1 hour 39 minutes to complete, exploring historic surroundings.

Q: Can I find loop trails in Alburquerque?
A: Many of the trails in Alburquerque are designed as loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33741485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful Cork Oak Forest loop from Esperança&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.3-mile (15.1 km) path that winds through serene woodlands.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Alburquerque?
A: Hiking in Alburquerque will take you through diverse landscapes, primarily characterized by historic castles, extensive cork oak forests, and designated conservation areas. The region's proximity to the Spanish-Portuguese border also adds a unique cultural blend to the natural environment.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the trails around Alburquerque?
A: Yes, for those interested in natural water features, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6545309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabroeira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the area, offering a scenic spot to visit.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Alburquerque?
A: The trails in Alburquerque are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the blend of historic sites, like the castles, with the tranquil natural beauty of the cork oak forests and well-maintained conservation areas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to explore while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Alburquerque is rich in history, featuring several castles and historical sites. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Campo Maior&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5549538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ouguela Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991593688/castillo-de-luna-circular-from-alburquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Luna – circular from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores the historic surroundings of the Castle of Luna.

Q: What is a good moderate hike for someone looking for a longer excursion?
A: For a moderate and longer hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna – Collado del Subterráneo loop from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans 7.9 miles (12.7 km) and typically takes around 3 hours 30 minutes, offering a rewarding experience through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any conservation areas I can hike through in Alburquerque?
A: Yes, the region features designated conservation areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32150128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caia Conservation Area loop from Campo Maior&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4-mile (7.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 52 minutes, allowing you to explore this protected natural environment.

Q: What is the longest hike available in Alburquerque?
A: The longest hike listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33742194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful Cork Oak Forest loop from La Codosera&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route covers 10.3 miles (16.6 km) and typically takes about 4 hours 36 minutes to complete, immersing you in the extensive cork oak woodlands.

Q: Is there a good hike to experience the cork oak forests?
A: To truly experience the cork oak forests, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33741485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful Cork Oak Forest loop from Esperança&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This moderate 9.3-mile (15.1 km) trail offers a tranquil journey through these distinctive woodlands.

Q: Are there any religious or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can find cultural sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capela dos Ossos de Campo Maior&lt;/a&gt;, a unique religious building, which can be incorporated into your hiking plans in the area.
tips:
Luna Castle, also known as Alburquerque Castle, is an impressive medieval fortress located in the town of Alburquerque, in the province of Badajoz, on top of a hill in the Sierra de San Pedro, dominating the town of Alburquerque and offering panoramic views of the Los Baldíos region. 
Built between the 13th and 15th centuries, the castle was expanded and reinforced by Álvaro de Luna, Master of the Order of Santiago and Constable of Castile, hence its name. 
Luna Castle is one of the most outstanding castles in Extremadura and a must-see for lovers of history and medieval architecture.
